title: What is the Temporal API in JavaScript?
description: Explain the proposed Temporal API and its advantages over Date.
options: Temporal is a modern date/time API replacing Date. Provides immutable types: Temporal.PlainDate, PlainTime, PlainDateTime, ZonedDateTime, Instant, Duration. Handles time zones correctly, supports non-Gregorian calendars, avoids Date's mutable pitfalls. Operations are explicit and unambiguous
correct_answer: Temporal is a modern date/time API replacing Date. Provides immutable types: Temporal.PlainDate, PlainTime, PlainDateTime, ZonedDateTime, Instant, Duration. Handles time zones correctly, supports non-Gregorian calendars, avoids Date's mutable pitfalls. Operations are explicit and unambiguous

title: How does JavaScript's structuredClone handle edge cases?
description: What types can and cannot be cloned with structuredClone?
options: structuredClone deep-clones most types: objects, arrays, Map, Set, Date, RegExp, ArrayBuffer, Blob, File, ImageData, and circular references. CANNOT clone: functions, DOM nodes, Symbols, property descriptors (getters/setters), prototype chain. Throws DataCloneError for non-cloneable values
correct_answer: structuredClone deep-clones most types: objects, arrays, Map, Set, Date, RegExp, ArrayBuffer, Blob, File, ImageData, and circular references. CANNOT clone: functions, DOM nodes, Symbols, property descriptors (getters/setters), prototype chain. Throws DataCloneError for non-cloneable values

title: Implement a JavaScript Proxy-based reactive system.
description: Create a reactive state system using Proxy that auto-tracks dependencies and triggers effects.

title: What are JavaScript Signals and how do they differ from observables?
options: Signals are fine-grained reactive primitives: a Signal holds a value and auto-tracks which computations read it. When a Signal's value changes, only dependent computations re-run (no diffing). Unlike Observables (push-based streams), Signals are pull-based with lazy evaluation and automatic dependency tracking. Inspired by SolidJS, Preact, Angular signals
correct_answer: Signals are fine-grained reactive primitives: a Signal holds a value and auto-tracks which computations read it. When a Signal's value changes, only dependent computations re-run (no diffing). Unlike Observables (push-based streams), Signals are pull-based with lazy evaluation and automatic dependency tracking. Inspired by SolidJS, Preact, Angular signals

title: Implement a JavaScript AsyncDisposableStack.
description: Create a resource cleanup manager using the Disposable pattern from TC39.

title: What is the difference between WeakRef and FinalizationRegistry?
description: Explain weak references and cleanup callbacks in JavaScript.
options: WeakRef holds a weak reference to an object (doesn't prevent GC); deref() returns the object or undefined if collected. FinalizationRegistry registers a callback that runs after a registered object is GC'd. They're complementary: WeakRef for optional access, FinalizationRegistry for cleanup. Both have non-deterministic timing
correct_answer: WeakRef holds a weak reference to an object (doesn't prevent GC); deref() returns the object or undefined if collected. FinalizationRegistry registers a callback that runs after a registered object is GC'd. They're complementary: WeakRef for optional access, FinalizationRegistry for cleanup. Both have non-deterministic timing

title: Implement a JavaScript lazy evaluation helper with memoization.
description: Create a lazy() function that defers computation until first access and caches the result.

title: What is the Iterator Helpers proposal in JavaScript?
description: Explain the TC39 Iterator Helpers proposal.
options: Iterator Helpers add methods directly to iterators: .map(), .filter(), .take(), .drop(), .flatMap(), .reduce(), .toArray(), .forEach(), .some(), .every(), .find(). These are lazy — they return new iterators without consuming data. Works with any iterable. Similar to array methods but for lazy sequences/generators
correct_answer: Iterator Helpers add methods directly to iterators: .map(), .filter(), .take(), .drop(), .flatMap(), .reduce(), .toArray(), .forEach(), .some(), .every(), .find(). These are lazy — they return new iterators without consuming data. Works with any iterable. Similar to array methods but for lazy sequences/generators

title: Implement a JavaScript CancelablePromise.
description: Create a promise wrapper that supports cancellation.

title: What are Import Attributes in JavaScript (formerly Import Assertions)?
description: Explain the import attributes syntax and use cases.
options: Import Attributes use 'with' keyword to pass metadata about imports: import data from './data.json' with { type: 'json' }. Tells the runtime how to interpret the module (JSON, CSS, etc). Renamed from 'assert' to 'with' in the spec. Also works with dynamic import(): import('./style.css', { with: { type: 'css' } })
correct_answer: Import Attributes use 'with' keyword to pass metadata about imports: import data from './data.json' with { type: 'json' }. Tells the runtime how to interpret the module (JSON, CSS, etc). Renamed from 'assert' to 'with' in the spec. Also works with dynamic import(): import('./style.css', { with: { type: 'css' } })

title: Implement a JavaScript EventEmitter with once and wildcard support.
description: Build an event emitter supporting once listeners and wildcard patterns.

title: What is the using keyword in JavaScript (Explicit Resource Management)?
description: Explain TC39 Explicit Resource Management with 'using'.
options: 'using' declares a disposable resource that is automatically cleaned up when the block exits. Uses Symbol.dispose (sync) or Symbol.asyncDispose (async, with 'await using'). Similar to Python's 'with' or C#'s 'using'. Resources are disposed in LIFO order. DisposableStack groups multiple disposables
correct_answer: 'using' declares a disposable resource that is automatically cleaned up when the block exits. Uses Symbol.dispose (sync) or Symbol.asyncDispose (async, with 'await using'). Similar to Python's 'with' or C#'s 'using'. Resources are disposed in LIFO order. DisposableStack groups multiple disposables

title: Implement a JavaScript promise-based semaphore.
description: Create a semaphore to limit concurrent async operations.

title: What is the difference between ArrayBuffer, SharedArrayBuffer, and DataView?
options: ArrayBuffer: fixed-length raw binary buffer, single-threaded. SharedArrayBuffer: can be shared across Web Workers for concurrent access, requires Atomics for synchronization. DataView: provides a low-level interface to read/write multiple numeric types from any buffer at any byte offset, handling endianness explicitly
correct_answer: ArrayBuffer: fixed-length raw binary buffer, single-threaded. SharedArrayBuffer: can be shared across Web Workers for concurrent access, requires Atomics for synchronization. DataView: provides a low-level interface to read/write multiple numeric types from any buffer at any byte offset, handling endianness explicitly

title: Implement a JavaScript pipe function with async support.
description: Create a pipe function that works with both sync and async functions.
